channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Lego expands Pokémon and interactive brick lines amid rising production costs
item.description: Lego is diversifying its product line by focusing on Pokémon-themed sets and interactive bricks as global uncertainties threaten to raise manufacturing costs. The company faces pressures from potentia...

item.title: Zanjoe Marudo shares his thoughts on ex-girlfriend Bea Alonzo's reported upcoming marriage to Vincent Co
item.description: Actress Bea Alonzo is reportedly set to tie the knot with businessman Ferdinand Vincent Co, the president and director of Puregold Price Club, following the public announcement of their marriage banns...

item.title: Tamil Nadu CM Stalin Writes to PM Modi Seeking Intervention on Commercial LPG Shortage Amid US-Iran Conflict
item.description: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M.K. Stalin on March 10, 2026, held a meeting at the Chennai Secretariat to review the possible repercussions of the ongoing US-Iran conflict on the state and plan appropriat...

item.title: Understanding How Leaders Can Support Challenging Team Members
item.description: In many workplaces, when someone is labelled as 'difficult', the usual reaction is to confront them or hope they leave. But often, the so-called 'problem employee' is actually a signal about the work ...

item.title: Hands-on first look at Apple’s new affordable MacBook Neo
item.description: Apple has introduced its latest low-cost laptop, the MacBook Neo, which promises to shake up the entry-level market. Designed to deliver strong performance at a more accessible price point, the MacBoo...

item.title: Zamfara Governor Dauda Lawal Joins APC, Strengthening Tinubu’s Support in North-West
item.description: On Monday, Zamfara State Governor, Dauda Lawal, waka comot from the opposition PDP and join the ruling APC. The APC France chapter hail this move, calling am bold and significant for the political lan...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Congratulates Iran’s New Supreme Leader and Expresses Hope for Stability
item.description: On Tuesday, Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif extended his congratulations to Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei on being appointed as Iran’s new Supreme Leader. Posting on X, the Prime Minister expressed his opt...

item.title: Associate nations impress despite T20 World Cup tensions and challenges
item.description: The 2026 T20 World Cup was marked by both off-field tensions and unexpected on-field performances. India, facing strained relations with Bangladesh and Pakistan, saw the tournament almost collapse bef...

item.title: Impact of Chinese Investment in Africa Depends on Governance and Oversight
item.description: China don dey invest plenty money for Africa for past 20 years, from mines, oilfields, railways to industrial zones. Dem don put over US$181 billion for infrastructure loans and about US$50 billion in...

item.title: Nairobi to Invest Heavily in Sewerage, Sanitation and Water Infrastructure Under Sh80bn Plan
item.description: Nairobi Governor Johnson Sakaja has unveiled a Sh80 billion development plan that prioritises sewerage and sanitation projects in the capital. About Sh33 billion is earmarked for expanding sewer netwo...

item.title: Cold Weather and Snow Showers Expected Across the UK This Week
item.description: After a brief spell of spring-like warmth, the UK is set to experience a return to colder conditions this week. A series of weather fronts will bring chilly air from the north-west, causing daytime te...

item.title: Man kills nephew over conflict during mosque prayer in Maguindanao del Norte
item.description: In Datu Odin Sinsuat, Maguindanao del Norte, a 31-year-old man named Polo Lindongan shot and killed his 20-year-old nephew, Benzar Lindongan, outside a mosque after an argument over the proper recitat...

item.title: Exploring Love, Trauma, and Healing in ‘Just For The Summer’
item.description: The novel 'Just For The Summer' by Abby Jimenez is much more than ordinary romance story. Eya, the story follows Justin and Emma, two people living far apart but connected online by a strange pattern ...

item.title: Rivers LGSC Chairman Unveils Legal Library to Improve Local Government Services
item.description: The Chairman of the Rivers State Local Government Service Commission (LGSC), Sir Israel N. Amadi, don yan say him dey committed to put the commission on strong ground. Him talk say through reforms and...

item.title: PM Shehbaz expresses Pakistan’s support and commitment to strengthen ties with Iran
item.description: On 10th March 2026, Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif sent a formal letter to Iran’s newly appointed Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, following the recent assassination of his father, Ayatollah Ali Khame...

item.title: Global Temperatures Approach Paris Agreement Limit Amid Extreme Weather Events
item.description: February 2026 was one of the warmest on record, with global temperatures reaching 1.49°C above pre-industrial levels, just 0.01°C shy of the 1.5°C target set by the 2015 Paris Agreement. The Copernicu...

item.title: Australia updates travel advisory for the Philippines due to terrorism concerns
item.description: Australia has updated its travel advisory for the Philippines, citing potential risks of terrorism due to escalating tensions in the Middle East. The advisory references a prior warning from the US Em...

item.title: Preparing for Sudden Advances in AI: Implications for Markets, Governance and Nations
item.description: Artificial intelligence is not a technology that improves in a straight line. Instead, it advances in jumps, with periods of quiet stability followed by sudden, disruptive leaps that can upend markets...

item.title: Researchers Develop Light-Controlled Protein Evolution for Dynamic Cellular Functions
item.description: Scientists at the Ecole Polytechnique Fédérale de Lausanne have developed a novel method called 'optovolution' that uses light to guide the evolution of proteins with dynamic behaviors. Unlike traditi...

item.title: Supreme Court directs tribunal of ex-HC judges to hear appeals against voter exclusions in West Bengal
item.description: The Supreme Court of India on Tuesday ordered the creation of an appellate tribunal consisting of former High Court chief justices and judges to hear appeals related to exclusions from the electoral r...
